dataset.description: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.

columns.description: Customer's email address used for communication
columns.name: email

columns.description: Customer's contact number in E.164 format
columns.name: phone

columns.description: Complete delivery address including street details
columns.name: address

columns.description: City or region where customer is located
columns.name: area

columns.description: Date when customer created their account (YYYY-MM-DD)
columns.name: registration_date

columns.description: Lifetime count of orders placed by customer
columns.name: total_orders

columns.description: Average monetary value of customer's orders
columns.name: avg_order_value

columns.description: Numerical rating (1-5 scale) for service quality
columns.name: rating

samples: Nothing to complain about, but nothing exceptional.
Delivery was fine, but the product could be better.

columns.description: Classification of feedback topic (Delivery, Product etc)
columns.name: feedback_category

columns.description: Unique identifier linking to order records
columns.name: order_id

columns.description: Timestamp when delivery was actually completed
columns.name: actual_time

columns.description: Difference between promised and actual delivery times (+/- values indicate early/late)
columns.name: delivery_time_minutes

columns.description: Delivery distance from warehouse/store to customer in kilometers
columns.name: distance_km

columns.description: Categorical reason code for delivery delays
columns.name: reasons_if_delayed

columns.description: Unique identifier for stock keeping units
columns.name: product_id

columns.description: Quantity of new stock replenished from suppliers
columns.name: stock_received

columns.description: Count of items marked as damaged/unsellable
columns.name: damaged_stock

columns.description: Monthly total of damaged inventory items
columns.name: damaged_stock

columns.description: Number of times campaign was displayed
columns.name: impressions

columns.description: Individual product price at time of purchase
columns.name: unit_price

desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Analyze sales performance across various time dimensions—daily, weekly, and monthly—to identify peaks, troughs, and seasonal patterns. Visualizing historical sales trends helps in understanding how promotional events, holidays, or external factors affect sales, thereby providing valuable insights for inventory management and operational decision-making.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal1_1742661777

effect.description: Brushing selects time range and propagates to other views

visualization.description: Line chart showing daily sales trends with overlaid marketing campaign events as vertical bars. LOESS smoothing line shows underlying trend
Gantt-style campaign timeline bars with line chart showing corresponding sales spikes below

views.components: MAD threshold slider for anomaly sensitivity

visualization.description: Stacked weekly area chart showing category proportions with MAD-based anomaly highlights
Streamgraph showing temporal shifts in category revenue contribution with dynamic aggregation controls

effect.description: Cycle selection for zooming into periodic patterns

visualization.description: STL decomposition showing trend, seasonal, and residual components in vertical facets

data.reason: The error indicates that a query which references `blinkit_marketing_performance.date` is failing, suggesting the column does not exist in the table. The requirement is to correct the SQL query to properly fetch the required data.
data.requirement: Correct SQL query for retrieving data from the `blinkit_marketing_performance` table, including the `date` column

visualization.reason: Visualizations referenced in the design requirements may be referencing incorrect columns. The requirement is to ensure that all visualizations correctly reference actual data columns in the tables.
visualization.requirement: Review and update the dual-axis temporal correlation visualization to ensure it includes comprehensive and correct data reference

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW daily_sales AS SELECT blinkit_orders.order_date, SUM(blinkit_orders.order_total) AS total_sales FROM blinkit_orders GROUP BY blinkit_orders.order_date ORDER BY blinkit_orders.order_date
sqls.name: daily_sales

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW campaign_timeline AS SELECT blinkit_marketing_performance.date, blinkit_marketing_performance.campaign_name, SUM(blinkit_orders.order_total) AS salesしゃtooltip
sqls.name: campaign_timeline

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW weekly_category AS SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', blinkit_orders.order_date) AS week_start, blinkit_products.category, SUM(blinkit_order_items.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM blinkit_orders JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_orders.order_id = blinkit_order_items.order_id JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', blinkit_orders.order_date), blinkit_products.category ORDER BY week_start, blinkit_products.category
sqls.name: weekly_category

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW category_revenue AS SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', blinkit_orders.order_date) AS order_date, blinkit_products.category, blinkit_order_items.unit_price * blinkit_order_items.quantity AS revenue FROM blinkit_orders JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_orders.order_id = blinkit_order_items.order_id JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id ORDER BY order_date, category
sqls.name: category_revenue

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW stl_decomposition AS SELECT blinkit_orders.order_date, SUM(blinkit_orders.order_total) AS total_sales FROM blinkit_orders GROUP BY blinkit_orders.order_date ORDER BY blinkit_orders.order_date
sqls.name: stl_decomposition

tasks.method: Aggregate daily sales using line chart with date axis. Overlay campaign events from marketing_performance as vertical bars with tooltip details (campaign_name, channel). Apply LOESS smoothing to identify underlying trends.
tasks.objective: Reveal overall sales distribution patterns and correlation with marketing campaigns through temporal alignment of events

tasks.method: Weekly stacked area chart showing product category proportions. Enable anomaly detection through MAD-based outlier highlighting on total sales volume hover interaction.
tasks.objective: Identify unexpected deviations from normal sales patterns across product categories over time

tasks.method: Dual-axis visualization combining campaign duration ranges (Gantt-like bars on top) with campaign-triggered sales spikes (line chart below) using synchronized date axis
tasks.objective: Establish temporal boundaries of marketing impact and quantify campaign effectiveness windows

tasks.method: Radial calendar heatmap with concentric rings representing categories. Cluster similar weekly patterns using hue saturation for quantity ranges. Integrate dynamic query sliders from I4 for category filtering
tasks.objective: Discover weekly purchasing pattern similarities across product categories while maintaining temporal context

tasks.method: Parallel coordinates plot linking inventory replenishment dates (from inventory) with subsequent order dates (from orders) using curved connectors. Color encodes delivery_status correlation
tasks.objective: Identify temporal relationships between stock replenishment cycles and order fulfillment performance

tasks.method: STL decomposition view showing trend, seasonal (52-week cycle), and residual components in vertically stacked line charts. Enable cyclical zooming through brush selection
tasks.objective: Isolate underlying growth patterns from recurring seasonal variations in sales data

tasks.method: Streamgraph showing daily revenue contribution per category using color-coded layers. Derive weekly moving averages through dynamic temporal aggregation controls
tasks.objective: Quantify category-level sales momentum and shifting revenue composition patterns
